name: Tulsi Tea
description: Tulsi tea may help provide warmth and comfort during a mild cold. It can be taken as a simple supportive drink when the throat feels irritated or the body feels heavy. Drink it warm and stay hydrated throughout the day. This works best with rest and light food.

name: Warm Steam Inhalation
description: Warm steam inhalation may help reduce nasal blockage and support easier breathing during a cold. It is a simple home-support method that can be used carefully for short sessions. Avoid very hot steam and take rest after using it.

name: Gentle Breathing Practice
description: When you have a mild cold, very strong yoga is usually not needed. Gentle breathing with relaxed sitting may support calmness and light respiratory comfort. Keep the session short and do not force the breath if congestion feels strong.

name: Hydration and Rest Support
description: For fever, hydration and rest are very important supportive steps. Warm fluids, simple food, and proper rest may help the body recover better. If fever is high or continues, proper medical advice is important instead of relying only on home support.

name: Light Herbal Fluid Support
description: Mild herbal fluid support may be useful when fever causes weakness and dehydration. The main goal is to keep the body hydrated and comfortable. Heavy exercise should be avoided, and rest should be the priority.

name: Honey Ginger Support
description: Honey and ginger are commonly used as gentle support when cough causes throat discomfort. This may help soothe irritation and provide warmth. Take it in small amounts and avoid anything that causes more throat irritation.

name: Steam and Warm Water Support
description: Warm water and steam support may help when cough is linked with throat dryness or congestion. These methods can be used as simple supportive care at home. Resting the body and avoiding cold exposure may also help.

name: Mild Chest Opening Stretch
description: A mild chest opening stretch and relaxed breathing may support comfort when cough feels tight in the chest. Strong practice is not needed. Keep the movement slow and avoid anything that increases discomfort.

name: Peppermint and Rest Support
description: For a mild headache, rest, hydration, and a calming environment may help. Peppermint-based support is often used for temporary relief and freshness. Reducing bright light and taking proper sleep may also support comfort.

name: Neck Relaxation and Gentle Stretch
description: When headache is related to tension or posture, slow neck relaxation and light stretching may help reduce heaviness. Avoid jerky movement and keep the body relaxed. Calm breathing can make the practice more useful.

name: Calm Environment Guidance
description: A quiet environment, reduced screen exposure, and short rest may help when headache is worsened by stress or overload. Mental relaxation is useful especially when the head feels heavy after too much work or tension.

name: Ashwagandha Support
description: Ashwagandha is often discussed in herbal wellness for supporting stress balance. It may be useful as part of a wider routine including rest, food, and daily calm habits. This should be taken carefully and not treated as a quick fix.

name: Anulom Vilom
description: Anulom Vilom is a calm breathing practice that may help reduce tension and mental overload. It can support emotional balance and improve the sense of calm when done slowly and regularly. Keep the breath soft and comfortable.

name: Grounding and Slow Breathing
description: Grounding and slow breathing can be helpful when stress builds up. Sit comfortably, relax the shoulders, and make the exhale longer than the inhale. Short calming breaks, less screen overload, and better sleep may also help.

name: Chamomile Style Relaxation Support
description: Mild calming herbal support may help when anxiety is linked with restlessness. The goal is not instant treatment but gentle support for calmness. It works best with quiet routines, grounding, and reduced stimulation.

name: Deep Breathing Practice
description: Deep breathing may help reduce anxious tension by slowing the body down. Sit in a comfortable position and focus on slow, even breathing. This can support steadiness and reduce the feeling of panic or restlessness.

name: Grounding and Reassurance Routine
description: Grounding may help when anxiety causes overthinking or panic-like discomfort. Focus on the present moment, slow the breath, and notice simple things around you. A calm routine, supportive rest, and reducing overload can help.

name: Warm Relaxation Drink
description: A warm relaxing drink in the evening may support the body when sleep feels disturbed. This works best when combined with a simple bedtime routine and less screen use. The goal is to prepare the mind and body for rest.

name: Evening Relaxation Yoga
description: Gentle evening yoga may help the body settle before sleep. Slow stretches, relaxed breathing, and quiet movement can reduce restlessness. Avoid intense activity and keep the session soft and calming.

name: Sleep Hygiene Guidance
description: Better sleep often starts with routine. Try sleeping at a regular time, reducing phone use before bed, and keeping the room calm. A slow mental wind-down can help the mind feel more ready for sleep.

name: Ginger Kadha
description: Ginger kadha may be useful as warm supportive care when cold and fever happen together. It can provide warmth and may help the body feel more comfortable. This should be taken along with hydration and good rest, especially when weakness is present.

name: Warm Rest and Fluid Support
description: When cold and fever come together, the body usually needs warmth, fluids, and rest. Simple support methods may help comfort, but the focus should remain on recovery. If symptoms rise or continue, proper medical advice becomes important.

name: Tulsi Ginger Tea
description: Tulsi and ginger tea may help when cold comes with heaviness in the head. It can give warmth and may support easier breathing and better comfort. Drink it warm, stay hydrated, and avoid cold exposure while recovering.

name: Steam Inhalation for Head Heaviness
description: Steam inhalation may help when nasal congestion makes the head feel heavy during a cold. It can support temporary relief in blockage and may make breathing feel easier. Use it carefully and keep sessions short.

name: Gentle Neck and Breathing Practice
description: When cold is linked with headache, strong movement is usually not needed. Gentle neck relaxation and calm breathing may help reduce heaviness. Keep the practice slow and avoid forceful breathing if congestion is strong.

name: Honey Ginger Warm Support
description: Honey ginger support may be useful when cold and cough come together. It can soothe the throat and provide warm comfort. Take it in small, suitable amounts and pair it with rest and fluids through the day.

name: Steam for Congestion and Cough
description: Steam may help when cold and cough create congestion and throat discomfort. It is a simple supportive remedy that can make breathing feel more open. Avoid overuse and do not use very hot steam.

name: Relaxed Breathing Support
description: Relaxed breathing and mild supported posture may help the body feel calmer when cold and cough are present. Avoid forceful pranayama and strong movement. Soft breathing and chest relaxation are better choices.

name: Fluid and Rest Comfort Care
description: When fever comes with headache, hydration and rest become very important. Heavy work, screen strain, and low fluid intake may worsen discomfort. Supportive care can improve comfort, but persistent symptoms need proper evaluation.

name: Quiet Recovery Guidance
description: A quiet room, lower screen exposure, and reduced mental strain may support recovery when fever and headache occur together. Mental calm is useful because overload can worsen the feeling of discomfort and heaviness.

name: Relaxation Herbal Support
description: When headache is linked with stress, calming herbal support may help alongside better rest and hydration. This should be treated as gentle wellness support and not as the only answer. Reducing stress triggers is also important.

name: Shoulder and Neck Release Yoga
description: Stress-related headache often feels heavier around the neck and shoulders. Slow stretches, neck release, and calm breathing may support relief. Keep the practice gentle and avoid holding tension while moving.

name: Mind Relaxation and Rest Breaks
description: Short mental rest breaks, slower breathing, and reduced overstimulation may help when stress contributes to headache. A calm environment and small pauses during work can support better mental balance and reduce tension build-up.

name: Calming Herbal Wellness Support
description: Gentle calming herbal support may sometimes be used in a stress and anxiety routine. It should be combined with better sleep, balanced routine, and relaxation practices. It works best as supportive care, not as a full solution alone.

name: Anulom Vilom with Slow Pace
description: Anulom Vilom done slowly may help when stress and anxiety happen together. It can support a calmer nervous system and a more settled feeling in the body. Practice gently without forcing the breath.

name: Grounding and Reassurance Practice
description: Grounding and reassurance can be useful when stress and anxiety increase together. Focus on slow breathing, present surroundings, and one small calming action at a time. This may reduce mental overload and improve emotional steadiness.

name: Evening Calm Herbal Support
description: When anxiety affects sleep, a simple calming herbal support may be used as part of an evening wind-down routine. This works better when paired with quiet surroundings, less screen use, and slower breathing before bed.

name: Bedtime Relaxation Yoga
description: Soft bedtime stretching and slow breathing may help when anxiety makes sleep difficult. The aim is to relax the body and reduce mental restlessness, not to do intense exercise. A short and calm session is enough.

name: Night Routine and Slow Exhale Practice
description: A sleep routine with slow exhale breathing, less stimulation, and a fixed bedtime may help when anxiety disturbs sleep. The focus should be on calming the mind gradually instead of forcing sleep.

name: Warm Kadha and Recovery Support
description: When cold, fever, and headache happen together, a warm herbal support drink may provide comfort along with hydration and rest. This combination usually means the body needs recovery time. Heavy effort should be avoided until the body feels stronger.

name: Quiet Room and Light Recovery Guidance
description: A quiet room, lower light exposure, and reduced mental effort may help when cold, fever, and headache occur together. The body often feels weak in this state, so simple recovery support is more useful than overstimulation.

name: Strong Warm Fluid Support
description: When cold, cough, and fever occur together, warm fluids and supportive herbal care may help comfort the throat and body. Rest, hydration, and monitoring symptoms are important. If symptoms worsen, proper medical advice is necessary.

name: Soft Breathing Only if Comfortable
description: In this combination, the body may already feel tired. Only very soft breathing support should be considered, and only if comfortable. Strong practice is not recommended during active fever or intense discomfort.

name: Steam and Warm Ginger Support
description: Steam and warm ginger support may help when cold, cough, and headache appear together. These can support throat comfort and make congestion feel lighter. Resting well and avoiding cold drinks may also help.

name: Neck Relaxation with Soft Breathing
description: Soft breathing and gentle neck relaxation may help when congestion and headache happen together. Do not do forceful breathing. The practice should remain short, light, and focused on comfort.

name: Evening Relaxation Support
description: A simple evening relaxation support may be useful when stress affects sleep quality. Herbal wellness can be one small part of a better night routine. The bigger focus should be calming habits and reduced late stimulation.

name: Slow Stretch Before Bed
description: A slow stretch before bed may help when stress causes restlessness at night. It can relax the body and make it easier to transition into sleep. Keep the practice gentle and avoid activating movements.

name: Sleep Wind-Down Routine
description: Stress can make sleep feel delayed or broken. A fixed bedtime, slower evening routine, and fewer distractions may help the mind settle. A short period of calm breathing before sleep can support better rest.

name: Calm Herbal Comfort Support
description: When anxiety is linked with headache, gentle herbal comfort support may be one part of care. The main benefit often comes from calming the mind, resting the eyes, and reducing overload in the environment.

name: Slow Neck and Shoulder Release
description: Anxiety can create tension in the neck and shoulders, which may worsen headache. Slow release movements and relaxed breathing may support relief. Keep the body soft and avoid any strain during practice.

name: Grounding for Tension Relief
description: Grounding and calming attention may help when anxiety adds to head tension. Reduce overstimulation, take short pauses, and let the breath slow down naturally. Mental calm can reduce the feeling of overload.

name: Night Calm Herbal Support
description: When stress, anxiety, and poor sleep happen together, gentle nighttime herbal support may be used as part of a calm routine. It should be paired with regular sleep timing and reduced stimulation in the evening.

name: Gentle Bedtime Breathing Flow
description: A gentle bedtime breathing flow may help when the mind feels overloaded and sleep does not come easily. The focus should be on softness and calm, not perfection. Short daily practice can support steadiness.

name: Full Wind-Down and Grounding Routine
description: When stress, anxiety, and poor sleep happen together, the mind often needs a full wind-down routine. Slow breathing, reduced phone use, lower mental stimulation, and calm self-soothing can support better rest and emotional balance.
